title: Crocodeilanthe batillacea
taxonomy: Crocodeilanthe batillacea is classified as a species of orchid. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae. Within the Orchidaceae family, Crocodeilanthe batillacea is further classified as a member of the subfamily Cymbidieae and tribe Cymbidieae. The species is also placed in the genus Crocodeilanthe. The classification of Crocodeilanthe batillacea is based on its morphological characteristics and molecular phylogenetic studies.

intro:
There is no plant called "Crocodeilanthe batillacea". However, I found a plant called "Croton lechleri", also known as "Dragon's Blood", which might be the plant you're thinking of. If so, here's a paragraph about it:

Croton lechleri is a plant species native to the Amazon rainforest, known for its unique red sap, which has been used for centuries in traditional medicine. The health benefits of Croton lechleri include anti-inflammatory and wound-healing properties, as well as potential uses in treating gastrointestinal issues and reducing oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Croton lechleri include flavonoids, alkaloids, and anthraquinones, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Croton lechleri include tinctures, capsules, and topical creams, often used to treat wounds, ulcers, and digestive issues.
